mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next month when policy is actively easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 2-year span, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future consumption, so equity prices should underperform over the next month after the trigger fires.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not significantly below untriggered returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and housing starts both weaken, the market reads a broad demand slowdown that reduces expected corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint downside in RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate above 4.5% tightens discount rates and financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should generate negative forward returns over the next week while policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if, during periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases or the conditional mean return is not negative at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production after a prior contraction indicates a rebound in real activity and profit expectations, so the S&P 500 should rally over the next month when INDPRO turns up on a short-term basis.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations after a decline
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of 20-day SPX returns is not positive in a majority of INDPRO rebound episodes, or if the conditional return difference versus non-rebound periods is indistinguishable at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payrolls and housing starts signals strengthening household income and construction demand, which should lift equities over the next quarter through better earnings expectations and sentiment.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-HOUST expansion does not outperform either proxy alone for predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no positive conditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is rising while the policy rate is still elevated, real rates and discount-rate pressure remain restrictive, so the S&P 500 should weaken over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates year over year while FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction between CPIAUCSL acceleration and elevated FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered instances show positive returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate near cyclical troughs supports wage income and spending, so equities should post positive returns over the next week when UNRATE stays near or below 5%.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE periods do not have above-baseline positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate for positive returns falls below 55% over any rolling 2-year window.
